name: ph-profanity-lexicon
title: Philippine Profanity & Offensive-Language Lexicon
description: Lexicon of profanity, vulgarity, slurs, euphemisms and minced oaths across Philippine languages plus English/Spanish loans as used in code-switched Philippine speech. Built for content moderation, NLP research, and linguistics reference.
intended_use: Profanity detection/filtering, toxicity-dataset bootstrapping, linguistic reference. NOT for generating harassment. Identity terms flagged context_dependent must not be auto-blocked.
license_recommendation: Data: CC BY-SA 4.0; code wrappers: MIT (see README)

severity_scale.1: mild - impolite interjections, teasing (log-only in most moderation)
severity_scale.2: moderate - clear insults; often playful among friends (review queue)
severity_scale.3: strong - vulgar anatomy/acts, strong insults, most slurs (block by default)
severity_scale.4: severe - mother-directed curses, gravest slurs and threats (block)

registers.formal: usable in formal/literary prose yet still insulting (e.g. mangmang)
registers.textspeak: chat abbreviation (reserved; textspeak forms mostly live in variants)

tgl.name: Tagalog / Filipino
tgl.note: Filipino (fil) is the standardized register built on Tagalog; entries apply to both.

eng.name: English (Philippine code-switching)
eng.note: English profanity as used in Taglish/Bislish, incl. localized spellings.

spotph.title: Spot.ph - Meaning of Popular Filipino Bad Words

bisdak.title: Bisdak Words - Common Bisaya Swear Words

gelhmo.title: Gelhmo - Hiligaynon Bad/Curse Words to Know

aninipot.title: The Aninipot - How to Swear in Bikol (6 common curse words)

fb-pampanga.title: Taga Pampanga Ku - Dana, Nakputa and other 'offensive' Kapampangan words

bienchabacano.title: Bien Chabacano - The F Word in Chabacano

model.title: Compiler knowledge (Claude) - native-level familiarity with Philippine usage
model.note: Entries citing only this source should get native-speaker review before production use.

normalization: fold diacritics (n-tilde -> n) but preserve original for display
collapse 3+ repeated letters to one (gagooo -> gago)
strip separators (spaces, dots, hyphens, asterisks) when checking multi-word severity>=3 phrases (t a n g i n a)
boundaries: require word boundaries for terms with length <= 4 OR severity <= 2
require word boundaries for ALL textspeak variants (pi, amp, bs, mf, sob)
substring matching only for severity >= 3 terms longer than 5 chars, and still subject to the allowlist
language_hinting: false friends (puke, buto, libog, agi, boto, burat, atay, supot) must not fire without a language hint or language-ID pass
code-switching is the norm: run Tagalog+English+regional lists together, then resolve conflicts via the allowlist
context_dependence: entries with context_dependent=true (esp. identity terms bakla/bayot/agi/badi/tomboy/bading) need hostile-context evidence (e.g. second-person attack frames 'ka'/'mo', toxicity score) before flagging
several expletives are affectionate among friends (anakputa in Kapampangan, buang in Cebuano) - prefer review queues over hard blocks at severity 2
phrase_exceptions: allowlist phrases always override token hits (leche flan vs leche)

allowlist.reason: rice cake (Sp. 'puto' is vulgar, Filipino 'puto' is food)
Spanish nickname for Jose; meme name

false_friends: 'boto' = vote in Tagalog/Bikol civic usage — innocent and extremely common

entries.notes: Bikol slang that spread nationally through memes and showbiz.

entries.gloss_en: your mother's vagina — the heavy Bikol curse
sex-act expletive used for frustration or surprise
'may you be eaten (by an aswang)' — ill-wishing curse
entries.notes: Invokes the aswang of Bikol folklore.

entries.gloss_en: female-directed 'chinga' derivative — offensive, though sometimes affectionate between close friends
cunt; damn (retains full Spanish vulgarity in Chavacano)
false_friends: Manila slang 'conyo/konyo' = posh rich kid — mild tease, not vulgar

entries.notes: Everyday frustration word; escalates in combos ('ataya sad nimo oy').

entries.gloss_en: gay man — neutral identity term in-group; slur in hostile use; also 'coward' taunt
entries.notes: Same moderation caution as Tagalog 'bakla': do not auto-block.
Recognized (and offensive) far beyond the Visayas.

entries.gloss_en: his/her mother's vagina — top-tier Bisaya curse
'chicken's vagina' — jokey minced form of the mother curse

entries.notes: Ranges from playful teasing to genuine insult depending on tone.

entries.gloss_en: 'struck by (chicken) liver-plague' — damn it / drop dead
entries.etymology: gi- (affected by) + atay (liver); references fowl pest disease

entries.gloss_en: strong exclamation derived from 'kayat' — roughly 'fucking hell'
dark/gloomy — used to call someone ugly

entries.etymology: From Malitong Yawa, a figure in the Panay epic Hinilawod; demonized under Spanish evangelization and turned into a curse
entries.notes: Playful among friends, strong in anger; ubiquitous across the Visayas and Mindanao.
'bish'/'beshie-bish' can be affectionate among friends — context matters.
Upper/middle-class Taglish uses raw English profanity heavily.

entries.gloss_en: motherfucker (less common in PH usage than putang ina)
ableist slur (borrowed intact into PH online speech)
gay man (Ilonggo/Karay-a) — derogatory in hostile use, reclaimed by some in-group
false_friends: 'agi' in Cebuano/Waray = 'to pass by / trace' — innocent
Tagalog 'burat' = penis (strong vulgarity) — same string, very different weight

entries.notes: Attested in an Ilonggo word list with an unclear gloss — verify with native speakers.

entries.gloss_en: son of a whore (from Spanish 'hijo de puta')

false_friends: Tagalog 'buto' = bone / seed — completely innocent; flagship cross-language false friend
'uke' in anime/BL fandom slang and 'uki' as a nickname are innocent

entries.gloss_en: your mother's vagina — THE Ilocano curse
entries.notes: 'ukinnana' is the third-person form ('his/her mother's...'); 'kinnam' is a clipped variant.
Dictionary-attested (Glosbe); 'ambagel ka lasi' is a common combo taunt.

entries.gloss_en: son of a whore — but culturally softened, an all-purpose Kapampangan exclamation
entries.notes: Kapampangans famously use it for joy, grief, anger, fear, even affection ('Lab da ka, anakputa!') — treat as severity 2 in clearly friendly contexts.

entries.gloss_en: your mother's private parts (recorded in a 1732 dictionary)
entries.notes: Historical/archaic; kept for linguistic completeness rather than live moderation.

entries.gloss_en: all-purpose exclamation ('oh my God'), euphemized from an ancient genital reference
entries.etymology: Clipped from an old Kapampangan vulgarity recorded in colonial dictionaries

entries.gloss_en: 'to hell with it / whatever' (from Spanish 'que se joda')
may your body be covered in scabies (ancestral curse)
may you burn / may you vanish (ancestral curse)

entries.notes: Borrowed Tagalog/Spanish insults take Kapampangan spelling (-u endings).

entries.gloss_en: clipped 'abnormal' — derogatory for people with disabilities; also a political taunt

entries.notes: 'amp' is the ubiquitous chat/text softened form (severity 1).
The 'anak ng <food/animal>' euphemism series is productive and playful (severity 1).
Innocent literal sense (pork/pig) is extremely common — context required.

entries.gloss_en: gay man / effeminate man — neutral identity term in most everyday and in-group use; slur only in hostile framing
entries.notes: IMPORTANT for moderation: widely self-applied and affectionate; auto-blocking it over-censors LGBT speech. Flag only with hostile context.

entries.gloss_en: term for Aeta/Negrito people — derogatory when used as an insult
entries.notes: Also appears in neutral community/place names; do not auto-block.
Literal 'baton' sense is innocent and common.
'itlog' (egg) is a jokey euphemism — innocent in food contexts.

false_friends: 'bobo' is a harmless word/name in Spanish and several languages

entries.gloss_en: penis (vulgar); in street slang also 'blind drunk'
false_friends: Hiligaynon/Ilonggo 'burat' = drunk (mild colloquial), not anatomical
'tsaka' (= and then) is an innocent near-homograph

entries.gloss_en: posh code-switching rich kid; pretentious upper-class mannerism (teasing, classist)
entries.etymology: Spanish 'coño' (cunt) — meaning shifted completely in Manila usage
entries.notes: In Chavacano/Spanish the source word is strongly vulgar; in Manila slang it is a mild class tease.
'gaga' is the feminine form. On KBP broadcast auto-censor lists. Escalates with 'mo'/'ka' or with putang-ina compounds.
false_friends: substring traps: 'magaganda' (beautiful, pl.), 'kagagawan' (deed), 'Lady Gaga' — use word boundaries

entries.notes: Formal-register insult — appears in literature, sermons, and editorials.

entries.gloss_en: 'animal!' — beastly person; also an expletive of frustration
entries.notes: Innocent literal sense 'animal'; profane only when directed or exclaimed.

entries.gloss_en: beastly person; son of a beast
to fuck; also an expletive of disgust
for Chinese / Chinese-Filipinos; dated everyday word now considered derogatory ('Tsinoy' preferred)
entries.notes: 'Intsik beho tulo laway' is a schoolyard taunt — severity 3-4.
Acceptable even in formal scolding registers.

entries.gloss_en: 'squatter' (informal settler) used as a class insult

entries.notes: Older/rural literal sense 'sudden jerk' survives in set phrases; assume vulgar in modern usage.

entries.gloss_en: 'low-flying dove' — press euphemism for a prostitute

entries.notes: Severity 4 when used as a directed threat (e.g. 'kakantutin kita').

false_friends: 'kiki' in ballroom/drag slang = a party/gossip session; also a common pet name

entries.etymology: Spanish 'me cago en la leche'; popularized during the Spanish era
false_friends: 'leche flan' (the dessert) is innocent — phrase-level exception needed
Cebuano/Bisaya 'libog' = confused — completely innocent; a classic cross-language trap

entries.notes: 'Malilintikan ka sa akin' (you'll get it from me) is a severity-2 threat form.
Very often playful/affectionate ('loko ka talaga!'); rarely worth blocking alone.

entries.gloss_en: flirty / promiscuous (gendered sting, usually aimed at women)
minced 'fuck' — dang; also a meme interjection

entries.etymology: Portmanteau of English 'fuck' + 'shit'; popularized by the 2002 film 'Jologs'

entries.notes: Also an innocent descriptor of things ('pangit ang lasa' = tastes bad); severity 2 when aimed at a person.
'pekpek shorts' (very short shorts) is established fashion slang — partially lexicalized.

false_friends: Spanish nickname 'Pepe' (for Jose); Pepe the Frog meme

entries.notes: Literal 'plastic' is innocent.

false_friends: 'pokpok' also means 'to hammer/pound' — innocent literal sense

entries.notes: Sometimes a neutral or proud self-label; hostile when aimed at others.

entries.formal_equivalent: ari ng babae / kaselanan (formal); 'vagina' as clinical loan
false_friends: English 'puke' (to vomit) is innocent — language context required

entries.notes: 'punyemas' is a minced variant (severity 1).
Severity rises to 4 when aimed at a person (esp. a woman) rather than used as an interjection.
false_friends: 'puto' (rice cake) and 'putahe' (dish, fr. Sp. potaje) are innocent
substring collisions: disputa, reputasyon, deputado, Putatan (place)

entries.gloss_en: your mother is a whore; as interjection works like English 'fuck!'
entries.notes: The strongest and most common Filipino curse; usually a non-literal intensifier (PH Supreme Court held it non-literal, 1969 & 2006). On broadcast auto-censor lists. Understood and used nationwide across all Philippine languages. Short forms 'pi'/'p.i.' need word-boundary matching to avoid false positives.
Literal sense 'mud' is innocent; expletive only by intonation/context.
'shocks'/'shux' are further-minced forms.
Literal 'to sip/suck' is innocent.
Tied to the 'tuli' (circumcision) rite; taunt implies immaturity.
false_friends: 'supot' = paper/plastic bag — an extremely common innocent word

entries.notes: Mildly irreverent interjection, not an insult; 'sus' now also mimics English 'sus' (suspicious).
Bare 'taba' (fat) is innocent; the -choy forms are teasing-to-derogatory.
'tangengot' is a jokey softened variant (severity 1).
false_friends: Spanish/Portuguese 'tanga' = thong; Malay 'tangga' = ladder

entries.formal_equivalent: ari ng lalaki (formal); 'penis' as clinical loan
entries.notes: 'etits' is syllable-inverted street slang.

entries.gloss_en: lesbian / masculine woman — usually neutral colloquial
entries.notes: Differs from the English sense; generally not hostile by itself.
Implies madness rather than mere stupidity; on KBP broadcast censor lists. Stigmatizes mental illness.

entries.gloss_en: 'monkey' — mocking; racist when aimed at ethnicity or appearance

entries.notes: Childish rather than offensive in most contexts; 'utot mo!' dismisses someone's claim.
Tied to the core value of 'hiya' (social shame/face); described as an ultimate 'loob'-wounding remark that cuts across social classes.

entries.gloss_en: gay man (colloquial; usually neutral, hostile only in context)
devil's mother — strong Waray curse; 'iroy' (mother) is itself rude in casual speech
entries.notes: 'iroy' is formal/literary for 'mother' (e.g. 'Iroy nga Tuna', motherland) but offensive in casual address — a register-flip unique to Waray.
